edges.context: Bandidos members shot and wounded a Rogues Gallery member.

edges.context: Bandidos members shot and wounded a Lepakko Gang member.

edges.evidence_quote: Hirsma reestablished his former club Undertakers MC after being expelled from the Bandidos that year, and also made contact with the Outlaws.
edges.context: Undertakers MC was reestablished by a former Bandidos president after expulsion.

edges.evidence_quote: In January 2008, two Bandidos and five X-Team members in Espoo were among twenty-one people charged with importing and distributing amphetamine.
edges.context: Bandidos and X-Team members jointly charged with drug trafficking.

edges.evidence_quote: This expansion provoked a war with the Hells Angels, who were already established in France.
edges.context: Bandidos expansion into France led to war with Hells Angels.

edges.evidence_quote: The Bandidos' first chapter in Europe was founded in Marseille on 20 September 1989 when the club patched over the Club de Clichy.
edges.context: Bandidos patched over Club de Clichy to form their first European chapter.

edges.evidence_quote: In June 1991, the clubhouse of the Wanted Bikers, a club in Haute-Savoie affiliated to the Bandidos but wishing to instead begin an association with the Hells Angels, was shot at and the motorcycles parked outside were destroyed.
edges.context: Wanted Bikers were affiliated to Bandidos but sought to switch to Hells Angels.

edges.evidence_quote: police, acting on intelligence supplied by an informant in the Buccaneers – a Hells Angels support club – arrested eight Hells Angels members in Grenoble in connection with the shooting, as well as members of the Buccaneers who supplied the stolen motorcycles used in the attack.
edges.context: Buccaneers are a Hells Angels support club.

edges.evidence_quote: Members of the Bandidos' Marseille, Montpellier and Metz chapters organized the 7 May 1995 shooting of two members of the Apocalypse Riders, a Hells Angels support club, in Créancey which left both men wounded and one, Jérôme Parent, paraplegic.
edges.context: Bandidos shot two Apocalypse Riders members in retaliation.

edges.evidence_quote: Four members of No Surrender's Metz chapter were beaten and had their colors stolen by members and associates of the Bandidos' Strasbourg chapter at a motorcycle rally in Pont-à-Mousson on 11 March 2018.
edges.context: Bandidos attacked and robbed No Surrender members.

edges.evidence_quote: During the arrests, a member of the Bandidos support club Bomber-Raiders was wounded after opening fire on police in Saint-Dizier; he was subsequently charged with attempted murder.
edges.context: Bomber-Raiders is a Bandidos support club.

edges.evidence_quote: The Bandidos expanded into Germany in June 2000 when the club "patched over" seventeen chapters of the Ghostrider's, Road Eagles and Destroyers that had been serving as probationary chapters since the previous year.
edges.context: Bandidos patched over Ghostrider's chapters in Germany.
